sections.paragraph:
ἐμφράσσω 1 aor. ἐνέφραξα (s. φράσσω; Thu. et al.; POslo 111, 130; 169 [235 a.d.]; LXX; Jos., Ant. 9, 36; 12, 346) to bar passage, stop (up), shut, τὸ στόμα stop the mouth (Demosth. 19, 208; Esth 4:17o; Job 5:16; Ps 62:12) of a monster (Da 6:23 Theod.; cp. Aelian, NA 14, 8 p. 345, 7) Hv 4, 2, 4.—DELG s.v. φράσσω.

aor. aor. = aorist

Thu Thu , V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

POslo POslo = Papyri Osloenses—List 4

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Demosth Demosth , IV b.c.—List 5

Theod. Theod. = Theodotion, revision of Greek OT, II a.d.—List 2, beg.

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Aelian Aelian , II a.d.—List 5

Hv Hv = Visions

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 326
